banner: Week 2 · Points, Lines & the Four Building Blocks
essential_question: How little does it take to pin down exactly where something is?
objective: Name and symbolize points, lines, rays, and segments, and tell them apart by how many endpoints they have.

content.prompt: If a point has no size, how can it tell you where anything is? Put one dot on the page and defend that it marks an exact spot.

content.heading: Point, line, ray, segment — sorted by endpoints
bullets: A point marks a location — no length, no width.
A line runs forever both ways (arrows both ends).
A ray starts at one endpoint, runs forever one way — write the endpoint first.
A segment has two endpoints — it is finite and measurable.

content.heading: Move the points; watch the figure change
content.note: On the sim, drag A and B. Toggle segment / ray / line and say aloud what each endpoint is doing.

content.preview: Build session: string-and-nail a line, ray, and segment you can walk along.

success_criteria.text: I can name a point, line, ray, and segment and write each one's symbol
I can tell figures apart by how many endpoints they have
I can explain why ray AB and ray BA are different but segment AB and BA are the same

questions.prompt: A figure with exactly one endpoint is a…
questions.explain: A ray starts at one endpoint and runs forever in one direction.

questions.prompt: Which figure is measurable (has a finite length)?
questions.explain: A segment has two endpoints, so its length is finite and measurable.

questions.prompt: Write the symbol for the ray that starts at A and passes through B.
questions.answer: ray AB (endpoint A first, one-way arrow over AB)
questions.explain: A ray is written endpoint-first with a one-way arrow: ray AB starts at A and goes through B.

questions.prompt: On the grid, draw segment CD and mark both endpoints.
questions.answer: A straight segment with C and D labeled at its two ends.
questions.explain: Rubric: a straight segment (1 pt) with both endpoints clearly labeled C and D (1 pt).

questions.prompt: How many points lie on any line?
questions.explain: A line is made of an infinite number of points — between any two there is always another.

questions.prompt: Is ray AB the same as ray BA? Is segment AB the same as segment BA? Explain each in one sentence.
questions.answer: Ray AB ≠ ray BA (different endpoints, opposite directions); segment AB = segment BA (same piece, direction doesn't matter).
questions.explain: Rays carry direction from their endpoint, so order matters; a segment is just the piece between two ends, so naming order doesn't.

questions.prompt: Exit: name one figure from your day (a road, a beam of light, a pencil mark) and say whether it is most like a point, line, ray, or segment — and why.
